headline: I started FarmThru because my kids deserved better than supermarket meat.
subhead: A letter from our founder on why we built a different kind of grocery store.
hero_copy:
When my eldest asked me where our chicken came from, I didn't have a good answer. I knew it came from a plastic tray at the supermarket. I didn't know the farm. I didn't know how it was raised. I didn't know how far it had travelled.

That question stuck with me. And eventually, it became FarmThru.

sections.heading: The problem I couldn't unsee
sections.body:
The average piece of supermarket beef travels 2,400km before it reaches the shelf. It sits in cold storage for 14-21 days. The farmer who raised it earns a fraction of what you pay.

I kept thinking: there are incredible regenerative farms right here in NSW. Farms where animals are raised on pasture, without hormones or feedlots. Farms run by people who care deeply about the land. Why can't families like mine buy directly from them?

sections.heading: So we built a hub
sections.body:
FarmThru connects 50+ regenerative farms directly to families through local collection hubs. You order online. Your food goes from paddock to pick-up in 2-3 days. You collect from your nearest hub.

No subscription. No lock-in. No warehouses or wholesalers in between. Just an honest supply chain where the farmer earns a fair price and your family gets food that's actually fresh.

sections.heading: 2,000 families and counting
sections.body:
What started as an idea has become a grocery store that 2,000+ families trust for their weekly shop. Rachel Ward, our co-founder, brings her own farm's regenerative beef through the platform. Bundarra Berkshires supplies heritage pork. Collins Wild Caught brings in wild-caught salmon from the South Coast.

Every product traces back to a named farm. That's the promise.

sections.heading: Join us
sections.body:
We're opening an equity crowdfunding campaign on Birchal in July 2026. For the first time, you can own a piece of FarmThru. Minimum investment: $50. Cap: $10,000.

Join the waitlist to be first in when the campaign opens.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: This is a pre-registration page for an upcoming equity crowdfunding campaign. No money is being raised at this stage. Any future offer will be made via a disclosure document on Birchal. This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice before making any investment decision.

creative_brief: Hero image: founder in a kitchen with family, natural and candid. Below-fold: the supply chain problem visualised simply, farm partner photos, hub pick-up lifestyle shot. Warm, personal photography. First-person tone throughout. Should feel like reading a letter, not a sales page.
